Title: Using native and synthetic genes to disrupt inositol pyrophosphates and phosphate accumulation in plants

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenic overexpression in whole plant; strong GFP accumulation in severe lines; signal observed in leaves, roots, and guard cell nuclei
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Inositol pyrophosphate degradation / phosphate homeostasis signaling
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream degradation step acting on signaling pyrophosphates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: InsP6 from InsP7 hydrolysis; InsP7/InsP6-related dephosphorylated products from InsP8 hydrolysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes inositol pyrophosphates InsP7 and InsP8, reducing PP-InsP pools; also hydrolyzes Ap5A and polyphosphate in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential macronutrient for plant growth; target for phytoremediation of phosphate-polluted soils
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Indirectly regulates target product by hydrolyzing signaling molecules that suppress phosphate starvation responses, thereby promoting Pi accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; acts as a strong control point for PP-InsP pool depletion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression in Arabidopsis and pennycress; metabolite profiling of InsP7/InsP8 by [3H]-inositol labeling and HPLC; in vitro enzyme assay with purified recombinant protein; Pi/P quantification; qRT-PCR of PSR genes; confocal localization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes phosphate accumulation by depleting PP-InsP signaling pools
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transgenic DDP1 overexpression in Arabidopsis reduced InsP7 and InsP8 pools in proportion to protein abundance. 2. DDP1 overexpression strongly increased shoot Pi accumulation and induced PSR marker genes including PS2, PHT1;4, SPX1, and PHO1;H1. 3. DDP1 protein hydrolyzed InsP7 and InsP8 in vitro and localized to nucleus and cytoplasm. 4. DDP1 expression in pennycress recapitulated Pi toxicity symptoms and elevated leaf Pi accumulation, supporting translational potential for phytoremediation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pi-replete versus Pi-deplete growth conditions; hydroponic culture; soil growth
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe growth reduction, leaf chlorosis/necrosis, embryo abortion, low seed recovery, strong Pi-toxicity symptoms; homozygous pennycress lines severely compromised
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering phosphate phytoremediation or Pi reclamation plants, but requires regulated/tissue-specific deployment to mitigate growth penalty
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phenotypes were rescued under Pi-deplete conditions; DDP1 effect on Pi accumulation exceeded that of ipk1 mutants and persisted over time

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgenic overexpression in whole plant; strong GFP accumulation in selected lines
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Inositol pyrophosphate degradation / phosphate homeostasis signaling
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream degradation step acting on signaling pyrophosphates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: InsP6 from InsP7 hydrolysis; dephosphorylated inositol phosphates from PP-InsP hydrolysis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes inositol pyrophosphates InsP7 and InsP8 and also hydrolyzes Ap5A and polyphosphate in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential macronutrient for plant growth; relevant to phytoremediation of phosphate-polluted soils
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Indirectly regulates target product by hydrolyzing signaling molecules that suppress phosphate starvation responses, thereby promoting Pi accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; native PP-InsP phosphatase affecting PP-InsP pool homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression and T-DNA loss-of-function mutant analysis; [3H]-inositol HPLC profiling; in vitro enzyme assay with purified recombinant protein; Pi quantification; confocal localization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression decreases InsP7/InsP8 and increases shoot phosphate accumulation; loss of function increases InsP7/InsP8 pools
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NUDIX13 overexpression reduced InsP7 and InsP8 pools and caused smaller rosettes with Pi-toxicity-like leaf phenotypes. 2. NUDIX13 overexpression increased shoot Pi accumulation, especially by 6 weeks. 3. Recombinant NUDIX13 hydrolyzed InsP7 and InsP8 in vitro, similar to yeast DDP1. 4. nudix13-1 mutants accumulated elevated InsP7 and InsP8, supporting NUDIX13 as a native plant PP-InsP phosphatase.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil growth over 3 and 6 weeks; Pi status context inferred from standard growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced rosette size and leaf chlorosis/necrosis, though milder than DDP1 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Native engineering target for modulating PP-InsP signaling and increasing Pi accumulation with somewhat milder tradeoffs than DDP1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously annotated as mitochondrial in some systems, but here GFP fusion localized mainly to cytoplasm and nucleus; mutant also altered InsP4, InsP5, and InsP6 pools
